authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(94) — Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.)
sources.quotedText: (94) Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.note: Milk Ranch Creek and its tributaries, above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River, are closed to all fishing all year.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River
regulations.verbatimText: Milk Ranch Creek and tributaries (Alpine Co.) above the confluence with the North Fork Mokelumne River. Closed to all fishing all year.

start.verbatim: Closed to all fishing all year

end.verbatim: Closed to all fishing all year

parameters.note: No open season; the water is closed to fishing at all times.

regulations.humanSummary: No open fishing season — closed all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Not applicable at this water since fishing is closed all year, but retained as the standing statewide requirement.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
